title: Campus Connect
subtitle: Building a student community platform from design to deployment
overview: Campus Connect is a responsive web platform that helps students discover events, join clubs, and stay updated on campus life. Built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript using a mobile-first approach.

problem.title: Problem
problem.content: University students struggle to discover campus events and club activities scattered across WhatsApp groups, notice boards, and social media. Information fragmentation leads to missed opportunities and low engagement.
bullets: Event information spread across multiple channels
No centralized platform for club discovery
Poor mobile experience on existing solutions

research.title: Research
research.content: Surveyed 25 students on campus about how they discover events. Found that 80% rely on peer sharing, and 60% miss events due to information overload in group chats.
bullets: 25-student survey on event discovery habits
Analyzed existing campus communication patterns
Identified key content types: events, clubs, announcements

insights.title: Insights
insights.content: Students need a single, scannable feed with clear categorization. Mobile access is critical—most students check updates between classes on their phones.
bullets: Feed-based layout preferred over complex navigation
Date and location are the most important event metadata
Quick filters by category increase engagement

personas.title: Personas
personas.content: Riya, a second-year student active in multiple clubs, and Dev, a first-year looking to explore campus life. Both need fast access to relevant updates.
bullets: Riya — Club organizer, needs posting and discovery tools
Dev — New student, wants curated recommendations
Both prioritize mobile-friendly interfaces

userJourney.title: User Journey
userJourney.content: Mapped flows for browsing events, filtering by category, viewing event details, and exploring clubs. Simplified navigation to three primary sections: Events, Clubs, Announcements.
bullets: Land on home → scan featured events
Filter by category or date
View details → share or save event

competitiveAnalysis.title: Competitive Analysis
competitiveAnalysis.content: Reviewed university portals, Facebook groups, and event apps like Eventbrite. Most solutions are either too institutional (slow, desktop-first) or too social (noisy, unstructured).
bullets: University portals lack modern UX
Social groups create information noise
Opportunity: clean, student-focused aggregator

wireframes.title: Wireframes
wireframes.content: Sketched mobile-first wireframes for home, event listing, event detail, and club pages. Focused on card-based layouts and bottom navigation for thumb-friendly access.
bullets: Home: hero + event cards + club highlights
Events: filterable list with date badges
Clubs: grid with member count and category

designDecisions.title: Design Decisions
designDecisions.content: Chose a clean, minimal aesthetic with generous whitespace. CSS custom properties for theming. Component-based HTML structure for maintainability.
bullets: Mobile-first responsive breakpoints
CSS Grid and Flexbox for layouts
Consistent 8px spacing system

finalScreens.title: Final Screens
finalScreens.content: Implemented responsive UI across home, events, clubs, announcements, and detail pages. Smooth hover states and transitions enhance the premium feel.
bullets: Responsive navigation with mobile menu
Event cards with category color coding
Club detail pages with activity feeds

prototype.title: Prototype
prototype.content: Live deployed version serves as the interactive prototype. All navigation, filtering, and detail views are functional with sample campus data.
bullets: Fully responsive across devices
Interactive filter and search UI
Deployed on Vercel for public access

impact.title: Impact
impact.content: Shared with 15 classmates for feedback—92% found it easier to scan events compared to WhatsApp groups. Project demonstrated full frontend development capability.
bullets: Positive peer feedback on usability
Demonstrated deployment pipeline knowledge
Portfolio piece showcasing design + code skills

learnings.title: Learnings
learnings.content: Mobile-first CSS saves rework. Semantic HTML improves accessibility and SEO. Starting with content structure before styling leads to cleaner code.
bullets: Component thinking applies to vanilla HTML/CSS
Performance matters—even static sites need optimization
User feedback early prevents feature bloat
